input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
       Title="MyMauiApp"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard – single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – two tabs inside one flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – single page -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. Let me provide the code directly since this is a straightforward scaffolding request.

Structure needed:
1. FlyoutItem "Dashboard" - single page
2. FlyoutItem "Products" - with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
3. FlyoutItem "Settings" - single page
4. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the files.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
       Title="MyMauiApp"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard – single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – two tabs inside one flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – single page -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the structure at a glance:

| Flyout entry | Content |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single `ShellContent` — no tab bar shown |
| **Products** | Two `<Tab>` elements → bottom tab bar with **Active** / **Archived** |
| **Settings** | Single `ShellContent` |

**Key points:**

- **`FlyoutItem` with one `ShellContent`** → no tab strip (Dashboard, Settings).
- **`FlyoutItem` with multiple `<Tab>`s** → a bottom tab bar appears when that item is selected (Products).
- **`Routing.RegisterRoute`** in the code-behind registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a non-visual route. Navigate to it from any products list with:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"product-details?id={product.Id}");
```

To receive the parameter, add `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` to your `ProductDetailsPage` class (or use `IQueryAttributable`).

You'll need to create the five page classes under a `Pages/` folder: `DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, and `ProductDetailsPage`.
